title: canthium dicoccum var. lanceolatum
intro: Canthium dicoccum var. lanceolatum is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family, also known as the madder or coffee family, and is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Canthium dicoccum var. lanceolatum include its potential to manage fever, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in the form of a paste to be applied topically, although modern preparations may include extracts or powders.
